title: Fireworks AI Seeks $15 Billion Valuation with New Funding - LinkedIn
full_text:
Fireworks AI Seeks $15 Billion Valuation with New Funding
This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
Rebecca Torrence
2w
Edited
Report this post
SCOOP: AI inference startup Fireworks AI is in talks for a $15 billion valuation with new funding, sources tell me.
The startup, which helps companies like Cursor run AI models more efficiently, last raised at a $4 billion valuation in October.
Index Ventures is set to co-lead the round, one of the people said.
This raise would make Fireworks the most valuable startup in AI inference.
My latest: https://lnkd.in/eFfzNxf9
73
2 Comments
Like
Comment
Share
Copy
LinkedIn
Facebook
Yichen Chu
1w
Report this comment
The market's valuation of AI inference efficiency continues to soar, highlighting a critical need for optimized model deployment. For privacy-sensitive industries and IoT at the edge, this trend amplifies the challenge of delivering performance while maintaining stringent security. We anticipate further innovation in distributed, secure inference solutions.
Like
Reply
1 Reaction
Michael Zellinger
1w
Report this comment
Well deserved! Best-hosted open-source models anywhere; their serverless options are the most reliable & convenient, eclipsing Together AI. Artificial Advantage is glad to contribute to Fireworks' success.
Like
Reply
1 Reaction

Report this post
$15.8B. Sierra valued on AI agent. Bret Taylor's AI customer-service startup Sierra closed a $950 million funding round at a $15.8 billion post-money valuation, led by Tiger Global and Google's GV, with Benchmark, Sequoia, and Greenoaks also participating. Taylor, who chairs OpenAI and co-founded Sierra with former Google exec Clay Bavor, said the capital is needed to maintain a lead in an increasingly crowded AI agent market, while forecasting a near-term 'culling effect' across the sector. → $950M raised. Tiger, GV led. → Taylor sees culling coming. Soon. The chair of OpenAI just raised a rival. What's your read?

Report this post
Came to Nice for a couple of days on unrelated matters, but used the opportunity to visit friends in Sophia Antipolis and join a small private meetup around AI products and infrastructure.
One thought stuck with me: AI features are becoming easier to build, but useful AI products are still hard to retain.
So the real question is shifting from “can we add AI here?” to “will people keep using this after the first wow moment?”
#AI #ProductManagement #Startups

What happens when you give innovation the space to grow? What started as an idea during an internal innovation challenge evolved into Prometheon : an independent AI startup focused on secure, locally hosted AI solutions. 🚀
In this video, Mario Beck and Pepijn Rinzema share how the Entrepreneurial Lab helped turn this idea in an actual startup, and why responsible AI matters more than ever.
Watch the story behind Prometheon below. 👇
#PeopleDriveTechnology #Innovation #AI

Report this post
🚨 One AI startup just raised $65B and is now valued at $965B.
🤖 Anthropic is now the highest‑valued AI startup on the planet, surpassing OpenAI.
💡 Why this actually matters
These are no longer “normal” startup rounds – they’re pre‑funding the next layer of AI infrastructure.
💸 Capital is concentrating into a tiny group of labs with massive compute and elite talent budgets.
For investors, the real question becomes:
❓ Who can still compete outside these mega‑funded players?
❓ Where do the durable returns sit when the top of the stack is this crowded?
Tell us what do you think 🤔💭 ?

What happens when AI starts building itself?|EXCLUSIVE:
Richard Socher has been a major figure in AI for some time, best known for founding the early chatbot startup You.com and, before that, his work on Imagenet. Now, he’s joining the current generation of research-focused AI startups with Recursive Superintelligence, a San Francisco-based startup that came out of stealth on Wednesday with $650 million in funding. Socher is joined in the new venture by a cohort of prominent AI researchers, including Peter Norvig and Cresta co-founder Tim Shi....

Came to Nice for a couple of days on unrelated matters, but used the opportunity to visit friends in Sophia Antipolis and join a small private meetup around AI products and infrastructure.
One thought stuck with me: AI features are becoming easier to build, but useful AI products are still hard to retain.
So the real question is shifting from “can we add AI here?” to “will people keep using this after the first wow moment?”

🚨 One AI startup just raised $65B and is now valued at $965B.
🤖 Anthropic is now the highest‑valued AI startup on the planet, surpassing OpenAI.
💡 Why this actually matters
These are no longer “normal” startup rounds – they’re pre‑funding the next layer of AI infrastructure.
💸 Capital is concentrating into a tiny group of labs with massive compute and elite talent budgets.
For investors, the real question becomes:
❓ Who can still compete outside these mega‑funded players?
❓ Where do the durable returns sit when the top of the stack is this crowded?
